# UC-15.3.21 · Access Control Panel Tamper and Line Fault

## Description

Enclosure tamper, RS-485 ground faults, or DC line faults may precede bypass or sabotage.

## Value

Enclosure tamper, RS-485 ground faults, or DC line faults may precede bypass or sabotage.

## Implementation

Alert at critical on tamper. Dispatch security to site. Log for forensic chain of custody.

## Detailed Implementation

Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Access panel diagnostics.
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `sourcetype="access_control:panel"` (tamper, line_fault, power_state).
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
Alert at critical on tamper. Dispatch security to site. Log for forensic chain of custody.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=physical sourcetype="access_control:panel"
| where tamper=1 OR line_fault=1 OR power_state!="normal"
| stats count by panel_id, fault_type
| sort -count
```

Understanding this SPL

**Access Control Panel Tamper and Line Fault** — Enclosure tamper, RS-485 ground faults, or DC line faults may precede bypass or sabotage.

Documented **Data sources**: `sourcetype="access_control:panel"` (tamper, line_fault, power_state). **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Access panel diagnostics. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: physical; **sourcetype**: access_control:panel. That sourcetype matches what this use case lists under Data sources.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=physical, sourcetype="access_control:panel". Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• Filters the current rows with `where tamper=1 OR line_fault=1 OR power_state!="normal"` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
• `stats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by panel_id, fault_type** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions.
• Orders rows with `sort` — combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.


Step 3 — Validate
Compare with the badge reader and access control system for the same doors, events, and time range.


Step 4 — Operationalize
Step 4 — Operationalize
Step 4 — Operationalize
Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Table (panels in fault), Timeline (tamper), Single value (panels compromised).

## SPL

```spl
index=physical sourcetype="access_control:panel"
| where tamper=1 OR line_fault=1 OR power_state!="normal"
| stats count by panel_id, fault_type
| sort -count
```

## CIM SPL

```spl
| tstats `summariesonly` count
  from datamodel=Authentication.Authentication
  by Authentication.user, Authentication.action, Authentication.src span=1h
| sort - count
```

## Visualization

Table (panels in fault), Timeline (tamper), Single value (panels compromised).
